candidates.why: Exact match to total revenues / sales; appears as the primary top-line revenue measure.
Represents operating income, not revenue; unsuitable for REVT.
Finance income, not operating revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Net income measure, not revenue.
Represents advances (liability) rather than recognized revenue.

variables.notes: Selected the main revenue line; duplicates of the same label are not repeated per instructions.

candidates.why: Best available proxy for direct operating/production costs in this E&P-style income statement; used as COGS equivalent.
Financial item, not cost of goods sold.
Specific overhead component, not the overall COGS bucket.
Balance sheet inventory, not an expense flow.
Subtotal including depreciation and other items; less suitable than the primary component line for COGS mapping.
Financing costs, not COGS.
Tax expense, not COGS.
Tax balancing line, not COGS.
Supplemental interest disclosure, not COGS.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Interest on lease liabilities, not COGS.

variables.notes: In an oil & gas context, 'Operating expenses' is the closest analogue to cost of sales/production costs; totals and financial/tax items are excluded.

candidates.why: Too broad; includes production-related costs and possibly D&A, so not pure SG&A.
Canonical SG&A-type overhead line (G&A).
Share-based compensation is an SG&A component (overhead), per instructions.

variables.notes: Selected specific SG&A-type components (G&A and share-based compensation) and deliberately excluded the broad 'Operating expenses' bucket to avoid mixing COGS and D&A into SG&A.

candidates.why: Represents capitalized oil & gas development assets, not a period R&D expense line.

variables.notes: No explicit research & development expense line appears in the income statement; ‘Development assets’ is a capitalized balance sheet item and not mapped to R&D expense.

candidates.why: Net interest line; suitable as a fallback if gross interest expense is unavailable.
Direct gross interest expense line; best primary choice for XINT.
Equity attribution, not interest expense.
Equity attribution, not interest expense.
Supplemental interest disclosure; use only if main interest lines are missing for certain years.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Interest on lease liabilities; narrower but still an interest expense component.
Equity line, not interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Provided a priority list: gross interest expense first, then net interest, then specific lease-interest and supplemental disclosures as fallbacks.

candidates.row_label: Realised (gain) / loss on listed equity instruments
candidates.why: Income statement line, not equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Unrealised (gain) / loss on listed equity instruments
candidates.why: Income statement fair value line, not equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Basic EPS on profit for the period attributable to equity  holders of the parent (USD)
candidates.why: Per-share performance metric, not equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Diluted EPS on profit for the period attributable to equity  holders of the parent (USD)
candidates.why: Per-share performance metric, not equity balance.
Appears to be synonymous with total equity including any minorities; less specific than parent-only equity which is also provided.
Component of equity, not the total book equity measure desired.

candidates.row_label: Total equity attributable to shareholders of the parent
candidates.why: Directly matches required concept: book equity attributable to parent shareholders, excluding non-controlling interests.
Total equity including any minorities; avoid to prevent double-counting with MIB.

final_choice.row_label: Total equity attributable to shareholders of the parent

variables.notes: Used equity attributable to shareholders of the parent to align BE with parent/owner equity and avoid double-counting non-controlling interests.

candidates.why: Income statement attribution line, but MIB is better sourced from the balance sheet when available.
Similar to 'Minority Interest'; again, income statement attribution.
Balance sheet line for non-controlling interests; best source for MIB consistent with BE selection.

variables.notes: Mapped MIB to the balance sheet ‘Minority Interest’ line so that BE (parent equity) plus MIB (non-controlling interests) reconciles to total equity.
